text: To connect your WordPress site to FlowBlinq GEO, follow these steps: 1. **Get Integration Instructions**: - Go to the **Setup tab** on your results page in the FlowBlinq GEO portal. - Click on **Get Integration Instructions** and select **WordPress**. 2. **Set Up Proxy Routes**: - You need to serve your GEO-generated files from your domain. This can be done using the `.htaccess` file or the WordPress rewrite API. - If using `.htaccess`, add the following RewriteRules: ```apache RewriteEngine On RewriteRule ^llms\.txt$ https://geo.flowblinq.com/api/serve/YOUR-SLUG/llms.txt [P] RewriteRule ^llms-full\.txt$ https://geo.flowblinq.com/api/serve/YOUR-SLUG/llms-full.txt [P] RewriteRule ^\.well-known/ucp\.json$ https://geo.flowblinq.com/api/serve/YOUR-SLUG/business.json [P] ``` - Replace `YOUR-SLUG` with your specific slug provided in the integration instructions. 3. **Add the Tracking Pixel**: - Insert the following tracking pixel code into your footer: ```html ``` - You can add this via the `functions.php` file or use a plugin like "Insert Headers and Footers". 4. **Update robots.txt**: - Allow AI crawlers to find your GEO files by adding the following lines to your `robots.txt`: ``` User-agent: GPTBot Allow: /llms.txt Allow: /llms-full.txt Allow: /.well-known/ucp.json ``` - You can edit your `robots.txt` using the `robots_txt` filter in `functions.php` or through a plugin like Yoast SEO. 5. **Test Your Setup**: - After implementing these changes, ensure everything is working correctly by checking the accessibility of the files and the tracking pixel. By following these steps, you'll successfully connect your WordPress site to FlowBlinq GEO! If you have any questions or need further assistance, feel free to ask.
